verses.text: Above the cherubims appears the throne, and the man in linen is told to take 'coals of fire from between the cherubims' and scatter them over the city. Fire of judgment. The same glory that blesses can also consume.

Above the cherubims appears the throne, and the man in linen is told to take 'coals of fire from between the cherubims' and scatter them over the city. Fire of judgment. The same glory that blesses can also consume.

Above the cherubims appears the throne, and the man in linen is told to take 'coals of fire from between the cherubims' and scatter them over the city. Fire of judgment. The same glory that blesses can also consume.
The glory of the LORD rises from the cherub to the threshold; the house fills with the cloud, and the court with brightness. The glory begins to move. Its slow departure marks the doom of the temple.
The glory of the LORD rises from the cherub to the threshold; the house fills with the cloud, and the court with brightness. The glory begins to move. Its slow departure marks the doom of the temple.
As the man goes in for fire, 'the form of a man's hand' appears under the cherubims' wings. Hidden power. Beneath the symbols of glory are hands that work God's will.
As the man goes in for fire, 'the form of a man's hand' appears under the cherubims' wings. Hidden power. Beneath the symbols of glory are hands that work God's will.
As the man goes in for fire, 'the form of a man's hand' appears under the cherubims' wings. Hidden power. Beneath the symbols of glory are hands that work God's will.
Four wheels gleam beside the cherubims, 'a wheel within a wheel,' moving straight as the spirit leads. Providence in motion. God's purposes advance with perfect direction.
Four wheels gleam beside the cherubims, 'a wheel within a wheel,' moving straight as the spirit leads. Providence in motion. God's purposes advance with perfect direction.
Four wheels gleam beside the cherubims, 'a wheel within a wheel,' moving straight as the spirit leads. Providence in motion. God's purposes advance with perfect direction.
Their bodies, backs, hands, and wings are 'full of eyes round about,' each cherub bearing four faces. Nothing escapes them. The ministers of God see all as they serve.
Their bodies, backs, hands, and wings are 'full of eyes round about,' each cherub bearing four faces. Nothing escapes them. The ministers of God see all as they serve.
Their bodies, backs, hands, and wings are 'full of eyes round about,' each cherub bearing four faces. Nothing escapes them. The ministers of God see all as they serve.

The cherubims rise — the same living creatures seen by the river Chebar — moving and standing as the spirit moves. One vision confirmed. God reminds the prophet that this is the throne he first beheld.

The cherubims rise — the same living creatures seen by the river Chebar — moving and standing as the spirit moves. One vision confirmed. God reminds the prophet that this is the throne he first beheld.

The cherubims rise — the same living creatures seen by the river Chebar — moving and standing as the spirit moves. One vision confirmed. God reminds the prophet that this is the throne he first beheld.

'The glory of the LORD departed from off the threshold,' and the cherubims lift their wings and rise at the east gate. The glory leaves the temple. The most fearful judgment is when God withdraws His presence.

'The glory of the LORD departed from off the threshold,' and the cherubims lift their wings and rise at the east gate. The glory leaves the temple. The most fearful judgment is when God withdraws His presence.

These are the living creatures Ezekiel saw beneath the God of Israel; he knew they were the cherubims, each with four faces and wings. Recognition. The glory that filled the house is the same that now forsakes it.
